what_it_does: This file is a channel-memory attachment record that links a design-source memory entry to an external image asset at image://design-source/company-info/p-07, storing only metadata such as id, channel, attachment type, target URI, and added timestamp.
observations: The file consists entirely of YAML frontmatter with no body content
It has an id att-4eb8ea95 and channel design-source
The target is an image:// URI pointing to design-source/company-info/p-07
An ISO 8601 added timestamp is present: 2026-06-22T11:30:59.663879+00:00
No code symbols are defined in the file
standouts: It records an image attachment rather than code or text
It uses an image:// URI scheme to locate the asset
It belongs to the design-source channel
conventions: External asset referenced by URI in target
concerns: No caption, alt text, or description of the image content
The image:// URI cannot be resolved or verified from this file alone
No version or checksum for the referenced asset
notes: A metadata stub that attaches an image asset (company-info/p-07) to the design-source channel memory.
questions: What visual content does the referenced image contain?
Is the image:// URI stable and retrievable by the consuming system?
Who added this attachment and what specific design-source entry does it support?
intention_signals: It appears intended to associate a visual design artifact with the design-source channel memory so the image can be retrieved alongside related design context.
novelty: Use of an image:// URI scheme inside a markdown attachment record; otherwise n/a.
contribution: Provides a persistent, typed pointer from a channel memory entry to an external image asset, allowing visual design artifacts to be associated with design-source context.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment metadata file (att-4eb8ea95) in the design-source channel that references an images-type asset at image://design-source/company-info/p-07 and records an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To associate an image asset with the design-source channel memory for later retrieval and contextual reference.
